metadata.label: r1
metadata.question: Extract birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon

output.question: Extract birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory from https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon
output.findings:
Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and died on February 24, 2001.

His foundational work, most notably his 1948 paper 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication', established the field of information theory. Key contributions include the definition of entropy as a measure of information uncertainty or 'surprise', the formalization of the binary digit ('bit') as the fundamental unit of information, and the development of the Shannon-Hartley theorem, which establishes the maximum rate at which information can be transmitted over a communication channel with a given bandwidth and noise level.

output.final_answer: Claude Shannon was born on April 30, 1916, and passed away on February 24, 2001. His three key contributions to information theory include: 1) The definition of entropy as a measure of information uncertainty or 'surprise'; 2) The formalization of the binary digit ('bit') as the fundamental unit of information; and 3) The development of the Shannon-Hartley theorem, which defines the maximum data transmission rate for a communication channel given its bandwidth and noise level. Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon
